findings.evidence: `BottomSubgroupFiberOrbit.lean:48` and `:60` expose both free-transitive orientations, `:114` and `:128` expose both regular quotientBot orientations, but grep finds only the non-regular quotientBot `apply_smul` at `:73` and no non-regular quotientBot `apply_inv_smul`.
findings.fix: Add the parallel `@[simp]` lemma `quotientBot_subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup_bot_apply_inv_smul`, proved from `subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup_bot_apply_inv_smul`.
findings.issue: The non-regular `QuotientGroup.quotientBot` API only has the `φ • e` form, so `quotientBot (subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup ... ((subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientBotEquiv).symm (φ⁻¹ • e)))` does not simplify to `φ`.

summary: The bottom quotient-group API is still incomplete for the free-transitive equivalence: the `QuotientGroup.quotientBot` specialization only exposes one of the two representative orientations developed everywhere else.
